# Sub 5: Layer 4 — structured wake metadata schema extension (DEFERRED)

## Parent Epic

#11829 — Sub 5 of 5. **DEFERRED per Epic AC6** until Subs 1-4 (Layers 1-3 + 5) land and residual idle-out is empirically observed.

## Premise

Per Epic AC6: extend wake-message schema with structured metadata fields so the receiving agent can program-against the wake context instead of parsing free-form text:

```js
{
  type                    : 'idle-out-nudge' | 'sunset-handover' | 'review-request' | ...,
  expectedAction          : '<machine-readable next-step>',
  currentLifecycleState   : '<agent's known lifecycle state from sender's POV>',
  suggestedQueries        : ['gh issue list --search ...', ...],
  currentSubstrateSnapshot: {openPrCount, unassignedCurrentReleaseCount, ...}
}
```

This is higher-lift than Subs 1-4 (schema change touching A2A wire format + every wake-sender + every wake-receiver).

## Deferred Until

Subs 1-4 merged + empirical observation of residual idle-out persisting despite Layers 1+2+3+5. Per Epic AC6: *"Higher-lift; defer until AC1-AC5 land and we observe whether residual idle-out persists."*

## Reactivation Trigger

If post-Subs-1-4 merge + 2-week observation window shows recurring agent idle-out OR receiving agents systematically misinterpret content-rich wake prompts (Sub 2), reopen this sub for implementation. Otherwise close as superseded-by-emergence.

## Prescription (when reactivated)

Schema extension to A2A wake-message format. Update:
- `add_message` MCP tool to accept structured metadata fields
- Wake-sender call sites to populate metadata
- Receiver-side parsing/dispatch on structured fields

## Acceptance Criteria (when reactivated)

- [ ] AC1: Wake-message schema extended per template above
- [ ] AC2: Backward-compatible (existing free-form-text wakes still flow through unchanged)
- [ ] AC3: At least 2 receiver implementations consume structured fields (proves the contract is loadbearing not over-engineering)

## Avoided Traps

- ❌ Implementing now without empirical evidence that Layers 1-3 + 5 are insufficient (Plan-agent over-engineering)
- ❌ Wake-schema-as-future-proof-substrate without consumer-side validation (substrate without consumer = substrate bloat per `feedback_truth_in_code.md`)

## Depends on

- Subs 1-4 (Layers 1-3 + 5) merged + empirical observation of residual idle-out

## Related

- Epic #11829 (parent, AC6 — explicitly deferred)
- Sub 1 #11905 (Layer 2 — must land first)
- Sub 2 #11906 (Layer 1 wake-content — likely covers most of the structured-metadata use cases via content-rich prompt)

**Authored by:** Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code)
